title_en: Introduction to Islamic Verdicts on the Pillars of Islam (Volume 1)
title_ar: مقدمة فتاوى أركان الإسلام الجزء الأول
content_en:
The five pillars of Islam — the declaration of faith (Shahada), prayer (Salah), obligatory alms (Zakah), fasting (Sawm), and pilgrimage (Hajj) — are the structural foundation of every Muslim's religious life. Yet the practical details of how to perform them correctly, what invalidates them, what is obligatory versus recommended, and how to handle exceptional circumstances generate an enormous body of scholarly discussion spanning the entire history of Islamic jurisprudence.

This collection gathers the fatwas (religious verdicts) of Sheikh Muhammad ibn Salih al-'Uthaymeen (1929–2001 CE), one of the foremost Hanbali jurists of the twentieth century and a member of the Senior Scholars Council of Saudi Arabia. Ibn 'Uthaymeen was renowned for his systematic analytical style, his ability to explain complex fiqh questions in clear language, and his grounding of every ruling in Quranic and hadith evidence.

Volume 1 focuses on the fundamentals: purification (taharah), the conditions and pillars of prayer, the rulings on Jumu'ah (Friday prayer), prayer in congregation, combining and shortening prayers during travel, and prayers during times of fear. The fatwas address both classical scholarly disputes and contemporary questions that arise in modern Muslim life.

Throughout, Ibn 'Uthaymeen demonstrates the balanced Salafi approach: following the evidence wherever it leads, respecting the classical madhabs while not being bound by any single one when the evidence points elsewhere, and making rulings that are both rigorous and practically accessible. This volume is an invaluable reference for any Muslim seeking authentic guidance on these foundational acts of worship.
